title: eriogonum ovalifolium var. proliferum
intro: Eriogonum ovalifolium var. proliferum, also known as Mormon tea or red sage, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Eriogonum ovalifolium var. proliferum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to be used as a herbal remedy, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
